strategy: Use the existing preprocess transcripts to extract the two referenced year expressions, then compute the sum and map it to the options.

steps.purpose: Read the relevant preprocess transcript spans to identify the year/style used for the rooms/bedrooms description and the founder-related year evidence available in the narration, then compute the sum and map it to an option while noting any ambiguity about whether the founder's death year is explicitly stated versus only another founder-related year being mentioned.

inputs.query: From these transcripts only, extract: (1) the year phrase the narrator uses to describe the rooms/bedrooms, quoting the exact wording; (2) the founder-related year evidence tied to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin, quoting the exact wording and stating whether it is explicitly a death year or only another founder-related historical year; then compute the sum of the two years and identify which answer option matches. If the death year is not explicitly stated in the transcript, say so clearly and report the best transcript-grounded candidate sum separately from any unsupported outside-knowledge assumption.

segments.text: This most unique member of the Dude Ranchers Association is laid out to look just like a town out of the Old West. But behind the doors of what appears to be the mining office, Miss Kitty's bordello, or Doc Holliday's dentist chair, are state-of-the-art rooms, decorated in an 1880s flair. We live in the newspaper. Juergen von Wolf and his wife Karen were here from Germany on a springtime visit to the ranch, their seventh in just five years, with plans for two more trips by the end of the year.

That famous town is just two miles away. We'll take you for a visit in just a bit. But even closer stands the namesake of Tombstone Monument Ranch, the 25-foot tall stone monument to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin. The Apaches ruled this country when the prospector came here looking for gold. Friends warned all he'd find in the desert would be his own tombstone.

And he came close one day when he hid out from an Apache war party in some rocks where that monument now stands, which you can visit on a trail ride. But when Sheflin discovered the silver strike that made him rich, he had the last laugh, naming his new town Tombstone, which would prove to be a fitting name. Here lies George Johnson, hanged by mistake, 1882. He was right, we was wrong, but we strung him up, and now he's gone.

expected_outputs.answer: option mapping or explicit ambiguity note
expected_outputs.supporting_points: quoted transcript evidence for both year references
expected_outputs.analysis: whether the founder death year is actually stated and the computed sum

refinement_instructions: Base the trace only on the quoted preprocess transcript spans provided to the tool. Preserve the exact phrase 'decorated in an 1880s flair' as the bedrooms/rooms year evidence. Do not claim the founder's death year is stated unless the transcript explicitly says so; distinguish 'founder-related year evidence' from 'death year.' If the tool finds that the transcript does not explicitly provide a death year, the trace should say the question is underdetermined from transcript evidence alone and only mention any option match as a transcript-grounded candidate, not as verified fact.
